title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS trusted by The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale the operational backbone of its GTM engine. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in RevOps, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, proven experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce expertise, and strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Experience with globally distributed teams collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ is required.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: Head of Revenue Operations aligns perfectly with Omri's target roles; 15+ years B2B SaaS experience far exceeds 7+ requirement; Fintech (cloud cost management) is in his strong fit vertical; Salesforce expertise and scaling experience beyond $30M ARR directly match requirements; Tel Aviv HQ with hybrid flexibility matches his location preferences.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations role and it feels tailor-made for your background—a cloud FinOps SaaS scaling with enterprise logos like NYT and Wiz needs exactly the kind of RevOps leader who's built GTM engines at scale in B2B SaaS. With your 15+ years and deep Salesforce expertise, you'd be walking into a CRO reporting line with the operational credibility to unify Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. The Tel Aviv-based hybrid setup also seems like it could be a natural fit given your location preferences.

title: Sr. RevOps Program Manager
description: Engine, a fast-growing travel and fintech platform backed by Blackstone and Permira, is hiring a Senior RevOps Program Manager to own the operational rhythm of its go-to-market engine. The role owns the RevOps program portfolio end-to-end—GTM launches, system rollouts, process changes, and enablement—partnering with Sales Leadership, Finance, and Product. It requires 5+ years in program management, RevOps, sales operations, or GTM operations. Engine operates a hybrid-hub model offering both office and fully remote options.
score_reason: Exceptional match: Senior RevOps role at fintech company aligns perfectly with Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS expertise, RevOps specialization, and fintech background. Program management ownership of GTM launches, system rollouts, and enablement directly leverages his core competencies.
ai_opener: Omri here—I noticed Engine is scaling its RevOps function under Blackstone/Permira backing, and your Sr. RevOps Program Manager role caught my attention. With 15+ years owning RevOps programs in B2B SaaS and fintech, I've built exactly what you're describing: GTM operational rhythms, system implementations, and cross-functional enablement that move revenue needle. I'd love to explore how I can operationalize Engine's next growth phase.

title: RevOps Expert
description: Remote, the global employment platform, seeks a fully remote RevOps Expert to optimize sales processes and system architecture. The role drives strategic initiatives aligned with the GTM strategy, champions scalable process design, and implements solutions using Six Sigma and Agile methodologies. Requires deep Salesforce proficiency, experience with tools like LeanData and Zapier, BPM methodology expertise, and proven SaaS/B2B experience. The base salary range is $51,100 to $115,000 USD, reflecting Remote's globally distributed compensation strategy.
score_reason: Strong alignment on RevOps expertise, Salesforce proficiency, and B2B SaaS background. Salary range is below market for a 15+ year senior executive, and the Six Sigma/BPM emphasis suggests more process-heavy than strategic growth focus, which may underutilize Omri's GTM and pipeline forecasting strengths.
ai_opener: I've been impressed by Remote's approach to building a truly global employment platform—it mirrors the GTM complexity I've navigated scaling B2B SaaS across distributed teams. With 15+ years optimizing Salesforce architecture and sales processes for high-growth companies, I'm particularly intrigued by how you're scaling revenue operations across your globally distributed model.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a revenue intelligence platform with Israeli roots, seeks a RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations to accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy and scalable territory design. The role partners with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition, redesign processes for global scalability, and ensure smooth GTM execution. Requires strong cross-functional collaboration and comfort navigating ambiguity in a fast-paced environment. The annual salary range is $149,000–$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, GTM strategy background, and B2B SaaS experience. Territory strategy, pipeline optimization, and cross-functional sales leadership directly match his core competencies, though the role is slightly more specialized in territory design than a traditional head-of-RevOps position.
ai_opener: Omri—I came across the RevOps Manager role at Gong and immediately thought of you. As someone who's spent 15+ years architecting revenue operations and GTM strategy in B2B SaaS, your track record with pipeline forecasting, CAC/LTV optimization, and sales process scaling feels like a natural fit for what they're building around territory strategy and seller productivity. Given Gong's Israeli roots and your location flexibility, this could be a compelling next move to apply your RevOps playbook at a revenue intelligence leader.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange for fast, affordable consumer/B2B research via an AI video interviewer. As the company scales rapidly in 2026, it seeks a GTM Engineer who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations to make the GTM team highly effective. The role replaces manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—including learning lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, activation onboarding flows, and time-saving AI agents.
score_reason: Strong GTM and RevOps background align well with the role's focus on automations, lead scoring, and attribution pipelines. However, the coding/engineering requirement is a significant gap—Omri is a RevOps executive, not a software engineer, which is central to this GTM Engineer role.
ai_opener: Conveo's mission to democratize research through AI resonates with the AI-native GTM playbooks I've built scaling B2B SaaS. While my strength is architecting RevOps systems and GTM strategy rather than hands-on coding, I'm intrigued by how you're automating lead scoring and attribution—these are exactly the bottlenecks I've solved through tooling and process design. Worth a conversation about whether a RevOps-first approach could complement your engineering roadmap?
